response: This code implements the Circuit Breaker design pattern, which prevents cascading failures in distributed systems by monitoring function calls: it starts in a "closed" state allowing calls, transitions to "open" after a threshold of failures (default 5) to block further calls and raise exceptions, and enters "half-open" after a timeout (default 30 seconds) to test if the service has recovered, resetting to "closed" on success or back to "open" on failure.
